description_long: This is a military-style helicopter with a streamlined, dark green fuselage featuring white markings and insignia. The helicopter is equipped with a single main rotor and a tail rotor, both of which are slender and appear to be made of metal or composite materials. The cockpit has large, slightly tinted windows that wrap around the front and sides, providing ample visibility for the pilot and co-pilot. The helicopter is supported by two sturdy, black landing skids with crossbars for stability. Visible on the fuselage are white alphanumeric markings, including '05', 'BLEND28', and 'VERTEX', along with a white star insignia. The helicopter is displayed on a circular, hexagon-patterned platform with a green rim.
description: A dark green military-style helicopter with white markings, large cockpit windows, and black landing skids, displayed on a hexagonal-patterned platform.
description_view_0: Front view showing the helicopter's nose, cockpit windows, main rotor, and landing skids directly aligned with the platform.
description_view_1: Angled left-front view highlighting the '05' marking, side cockpit window, and the 'BLEND28' label on the tail boom.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail boom, tail rotor, and the symmetry of the main rotor and landing skids.
description_view_3: Angled right-front view showing the '05' marking, side cockpit window, and the 'VERTEX' label on the tail boom.
attribute_rotor_blades: The main rotor has two long, narrow blades, likely made of composite or metal, colored dark gray or black, with no visible markings.
attribute_tail_rotor: The tail rotor is present on the vertical fin, featuring two short blades oriented vertically, colored dark gray or black, with no visible markings or protective shroud.
attribute_landing_skids: The helicopter has two black, tubular landing skids with crossbars for stability, appearing to be made of metal or composite material.
attribute_cockpit_windows: There are three large cockpit windows: one front-facing and two side windows, all slightly tinted and framed in green, providing wide visibility.
attribute_registration_number: '05', 'BLEND28', and 'VERTEX' are visible as alphanumeric markings on the fuselage and tail boom.
